topics.title: Heat reshapes U.S. holiday events
topics.summary: Extreme heat across parts of the Midwest and East Coast is forcing changes to July 4th weekend events as America 250 celebrations ramp up.
topics.discussionPrompt: Big public celebrations sound fun, but heat can change everything. Would you still go to a major outdoor event if the weather looked uncomfortable?

topics.title: World Cup heat sparks climate debate
topics.summary: Scientists say climate change is intensifying the heat affecting World Cup players and fans, renewing questions about sports scheduling and safety plans.
topics.discussionPrompt: The World Cup is supposed to bring people together. Should tournaments move times or locations more aggressively when heat becomes a safety issue?

topics.title: Country Roads becomes World Cup anthem
topics.summary: U.S. players and fans have turned John Denver's Country Roads into an unofficial World Cup anthem, singing it after matches during the team's run.
topics.discussionPrompt: It is funny how one song can suddenly belong to a whole crowd. What song would make the best shared anthem after a big win?

topics.title: U.S. World Cup match sets TV record
topics.summary: The U.S. win over Bosnia-Herzegovina became the most-watched English-language soccer telecast in history, according to Fox Sports figures reported by AP.
topics.discussionPrompt: Soccer keeps getting bigger in North America. Do you think a home World Cup can turn casual viewers into long-term fans?

topics.title: Switzerland advances at World Cup
topics.summary: Switzerland beat Algeria 2-0 in Vancouver, moving into the World Cup last 16 with a controlled performance and another strong outing from Johan Manzambi.
topics.discussionPrompt: World Cups always seem to create breakout players and surprise runs. Is that the best part of the tournament?
